action: The go.yml `test (race + cover, incl. integration tier)` step intermittently FAILs on internal/bridge real-tmux / fake-CLI integration tests in CI (ubuntu-latest + macos-latest): symptoms '[claude-tmux] FAIL: REPL prompt never appeared after 60s', '[claude-tmux] FAIL: prompt spilled into a dead shell (zsh) after paste - CLI process gone (cycle-274 class)', '[itest-tmux] FAIL: completion never signalled', and '--- FAIL: TestExecRunner_WritesPIDFile'. PROVEN FLAKY (not a regression): internal/bridge is byte-identical to the green release commit 56deec69 (the driver-agnostic cycles changed only *_test.go + JSON profiles + docs), the same code passed CI on 56deec69, it passes locally (`go test -tags integration ./internal/bridge/` -> ok 20.753s), and CI run 27507238943 FAILED on the integration tier then PASSED on `gh run rerun --failed` with zero code change. Cost: a CI re-run is required on most pushes that touch the go tree. FIX: stabilize or quarantine the environment-sensitive real-tmux tests - raise the REPL-appearance timeout for CI (fake-CLI under a loaded runner), move the flakiest TestRealTmux_* / TestEscalationFinalPane* / TestPostPasteShellSpillFailsFast / TestExecRunner_WritesPIDFile into a separate non-blocking lane, or add a bounded retry for the REPL-prompt handshake in CI.
evidence_pointer: go workflow run 27507238943 (failed integration tier, then success on rerun, HEAD 965009c9); local `go test -tags integration ./internal/bridge/` = ok 20.753s
